summary: PR #2657 is an open GR/stage decompilation slice touching five src/melee/gr files. The PR body claims exact-match decompilation of 5 files with 4 newly matched functions, and the bot report lists new matches for grCastle_801CD658, grKongo_801D7BBC, grIceMt_801F9ACC, and grVenom_80204284 plus 10 unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Matched/improved Castle stage functions; changed loop initialization, platform state update access shape, declaration/lifetime shape, and compound assignment.
evidence_refs: diff: grCastle_801CD658 initializes dynamics[0..11] with a single for loop and casts loop comparison to u32
diff: grCastle_801CDC44 rewrites platform access through gp->gv.castle8.plat[0] while advancing gp by platform stride
bot_report: grCastle_801CD658 new match; grCastle_801CDC44, grCastle_801CF868, grCastle_801CE3AC improved

key_files.role: Small Great Bay matching improvement by splitting a direct hsd_obj assignment through a temporary.
evidence_refs: diff: grGreatBay_801F5460 adds jobj_tmp before assigning jobj

key_files.role: Ice Mountain matching work; declaration order, pointer/list filling, assert expansion, direct call shape, and a small inline getter were adjusted.
evidence_refs: diff: grIceMt_801F8CDC reorders int i and archive declarations
diff: fn_801F8E58 replaces timer pointer with (&gp->gv.icemt.xDC)[chosen] and fills list through s32* p
diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC expands several HSD_ASSERT calls into explicit __assert checks and calls grIceMt_801F71E8 instead of grIceMt_801F71E8_noinline2
diff: grIceMt_801FA500 adds static inline grIceMt_801FA500_get_jobj
bot_report: grIceMt_801F9ACC new match; grIceMt_801FA500, fn_801F8E58, grIceMt_801F8CDC improved

key_files.role: Kongo stage matching work; added extern float constants, adjusted assert string source, probability/control-flow arithmetic, declaration scope, and switch case local reuse.
evidence_refs: diff: adds extern const f32 grKg_804DAFA0 and grKg_804DAFA4
diff: grKongo_801D828C changes assert message argument from literal to grKg_803E1A00
diff: grKongo_801D7BBC rewrites random/probability arithmetic using grKg_804DAFA0/grKg_804DAFA4 and shared HSD_GObj* obj
bot_report: grKongo_801D7BBC new match; .data and grKongo_801D7134 improved

key_files.role: Venom stage matching work; adjusted stack padding, temporaries, ternary assignment, random range computation, and object assignment shape.
evidence_refs: diff: grVenom_80203B18 adds pad8, zero, PAD_STACK(0xC), ternary flag assignment, diff temporaries, and assignment in condition for lobj
diff excerpt: grVenom_80204284 introduces tmp_gp before gp assignment
bot_report: grVenom_80204284 new match; grVenom_80203B18 improved

what_changed: The PR touched five GR source files under src/melee/gr and reports 151 added and 134 deleted lines in the metadata.
Four functions are reported as newly matched by the bot: grCastle_801CD658, grKongo_801D7BBC, grIceMt_801F9ACC, and grVenom_80204284.

Ten additional items are reported as improved but not fully matched, including grVenom_80203B18, grCastle_801CDC44, grIceMt_801FA500, grCastle_801CF868, grGreatBay_801F5460, fn_801F8E58, and grIceMt_801F8CDC.

The diff uses a mix of source-shape changes: natural loop consolidation, declaration order changes, temporary variables, explicit assert expansions, small inline helper introduction, pointer/array access reshaping, ternary assignment, compound assignment, and stack padding.

smart_moves.text: Consolidated repeated NULL assignments in grCastle_801CD658 into a single loop over dynamics[0..11], matching the loaded natural-loops standard's preference for loop forms when repeated blocks differ only by index.
evidence_refs: diff: grCastle_801CD658 replaces dynamics[0] through dynamics[7] manual assignments plus loop from 8 with for (i = 0; 12 > i; i++)

smart_moves.text: Kept several matching adjustments narrow and local to the affected functions, such as adding a temporary jobj variable in grGreatBay_801F5460 and tmp_gp in grVenom_80204284 rather than broad rewrites.
evidence_refs: bot_report: both functions improved or newly matched

smart_moves.text: Used explicit float constant externs in grKongo_801D7BBC where the diff suggests exact literal/data shape mattered for matching, while keeping the change limited to grkongo.c.
evidence_refs: diff: extern const f32 grKg_804DAFA0/grKg_804DAFA4 added
diff: grKongo_801D7BBC compares against grKg_804DAFA0 and uses grKg_804DAFA4 - grKg_804D6980->unk18

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated field initialization is present, testing a loop that covers the full range can unlock an exact match and improve source readability.
For MWCC matching in GR functions, declaration order and local lifetime/temporary shape can matter even when semantics are unchanged.
evidence_refs: diff: grIceMt_801F8CDC reorders int i and UnkArchiveStruct* archive
diff: grCastle_801CE3AC moves HSD_JObj* jobj declaration and removes PAD_STACK(4)

decomp_lessons.lesson: A pointer-walk or base-plus-index expression may match better than a named pointer variable for adjacent struct fields, as shown by fn_801F8E58 changing timer[chosen] into (&gp->gv.icemt.xDC)[chosen].
evidence_refs: diff: fn_801F8E58 removes s16* timer and uses (&gp->gv.icemt.xDC)[chosen]

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert macro cleanup is not always the final matching form; this PR's Ice Mountain function expanded several HSD_ASSERT uses back into explicit __assert checks with file symbols and messages.
evidence_refs: diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC replaces HSD_ASSERT(0xAB9, mgobj) with if (mgobj == NULL) __assert(grIm_803E46F8, 0xAB9, "mgobj")

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary names in this slice are mechanical and role-local rather than semantic: jobj_tmp, tmp_gp, zero, diff, delta, rand, p, obj.
evidence_refs: diff: grVenom_80203B18 uses zero and diff
diff: grKongo_801D7BBC uses delta, rand, zero, obj

naming_conventions.convention: New helper grIceMt_801FA500_get_jobj follows the address-style function stem plus role suffix pattern, avoiding a broader semantic rename.
evidence_refs: diff: static inline HSD_JObj* grIceMt_801FA500_get_jobj(HSD_GObj* gobj)

naming_conventions.convention: New external float symbols retain address-style names grKg_804DAFA0 and grKg_804DAFA4 rather than guessed semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact loop comparison spelling and casts when MWCC loop shape matters, such as for (i = 0; 12 > i; i++) and (u32) i < 4U.
Reshape array/struct traversal by keeping index zero accesses and advancing the containing pointer by a struct stride when needed for codegen.
evidence_refs: diff: grCastle_801CDC44 accesses gp->gv.castle8.plat[0] and advances gp = (Ground*) ((struct grCastle_Platform*) gp + 1)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce or remove stack padding only in narrow functions where the report shows matching progress; this slice uses PAD_STACK in grCastle_801CDC44 and grVenom_80203B18 and removes PAD_STACK(4) from grCastle_801CE3AC.
evidence_refs: diff: grVenom_80203B18 adds PAD_STACK(0xC) and u8 pad8[8]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split direct expressions into named temporaries to influence evaluation order and register/stack lifetimes, as seen with rand, delta, diff, zero, jobj_tmp, and tmp_gp.
evidence_refs: diff: grKongo_801D7BBC introduces rand and delta
diff: grVenom_80203B18 introduces diff and zero

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicit __assert blocks may be necessary when HSD_ASSERT macro form does not reproduce the original assert codegen or string/file reference shape.
evidence_refs: diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC expands multiple HSD_ASSERT calls to explicit __assert

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A tiny static inline accessor can affect matching even when it simply returns a struct field.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that review or revert explicit __assert expansions in src/melee/gr/gricemt.c
Search for grCastle_801CDC44 platform pointer-stride patterns in later GR matching work
Search for grKg_804DAFA0 grKg_804DAFA4 data ownership or symbol metadata changes
Find subsequent changes to grIceMt_801FA500_get_jobj to see whether the inline helper was kept
Search for PRs touching grVenom_80204284 after PR #2657

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: exact-match summary and verification claims
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with 4 new matches and 10 improvements
raw_diff/diff_excerpt: concrete code changes in all five files
notes: No review comments or reviews are loaded; review_feedback is empty rather than inferred.
The raw diff is truncated in loaded_files and diff_excerpt, so grvenom.c later hunks are only partially visible.
Verification claims come from the PR body and bot report in the slice; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
PR state is OPEN and merged_at is null in the loaded metadata; no merge or acceptance status is inferred.
Some tactics, especially stack padding and explicit assert expansion, are supported as PR-local matching tactics but should not be promoted as general style without curator review.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Loop consolidation can be a matching move for repeated GR state initialization
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2657, grCastle_801CD658 replaced repeated dynamics[i].data = NULL assignments with a single loop over i = 0..11 and the bot reported the function as a new exact match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Explicit assert expansion may be match-relevant in GR code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2657, grIceMt_801F9ACC changed multiple HSD_ASSERT macro uses into explicit null checks calling __assert with grIm_803E46F8 and literal messages, and the bot reported grIceMt_801F9ACC as a new exact match. Treat this as PR-local evidence, not a blanket preference over assert macros.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Temporary splitting influenced GR function improvements
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several improved functions in PR #2657 split direct expressions through local temporaries, including jobj_tmp, tmp_gp, zero, diff, delta, rand, and p. This is evidence for trying narrow temporary/lifetime changes during MWCC matching, with verification required.
evidence_refs: bot_report: affected functions improved or matched

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2657 touched five GR stage source files
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2657 changed src/melee/gr/grcastle.c, grgreatbay.c, gricemt.c, grkongo.c, and grvenom.c for GR/stage decomp matching, with bot-reported new matches in Castle, Kongo, Ice Mountain, and Venom functions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-scoped retrieval for GR/stage matching history; curator should decide whether PR-local history belongs in path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize assert macro preference
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2657 is a counterexample where explicit __assert blocks replaced HSD_ASSERT and the function matched. If standards mention preferring assert macros, they may need a caveat that explicit __assert can be match-required when file symbol/message/codegen shape proves it.
evidence_refs: diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC HSD_ASSERT to explicit __assert changes
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded assert-report-macros standard generally prefers macros; this PR provides PR-local evidence for a narrow exception, but curator review is needed before modifying the global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Stack padding and tiny helper tactics require verification and narrow scope
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2657 uses PAD_STACK, an unused pad array, temporary split variables, and a trivial static inline getter in functions that improved. This supports the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard rather than requiring a new standard.
evidence_refs: diff: grVenom_80203B18 adds u8 pad8[8] and PAD_STACK(0xC)
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reinforcement/example for an existing standard; not enough to add a broad new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer acceptance or reviewer intent; no reviews or review comments are loaded.
Do not promote explicit __assert as a general preferred style; the loaded standards prefer macros when they match, and this PR only supports a local matching exception.
Do not claim all five files are fully matched; the PR body says exact-match decompilation of 5 files but the bot specifically reports 4 new matches and 10 improvements, some still below 100%.
Do not infer semantic meanings for address-style globals grKg_804DAFA0/grKg_804DAFA4 or functions with address-style names.
